rubric_items.reasoning: The final state is verified via authoritative rabbitmqctl output, not just agent self-report: app-user shows exactly one permission row on /app, ops-user exactly one row on /ops, matching the required end-state precisely. Cluster shows 3/3 disk+running nodes. Both clients are 1/1 Running with 0 restarts. The agent went beyond the surface permission fix to resolve a genuine second-layer blocker (a queue-argument mismatch left over from the prior stage) by patching the Deployment's declare arguments to match the existing queue rather than deleting/recreating it, preserving the 383 messages already in app-queue. This is a root-cause fix, not a symptom patch, and the oracle passed. Slight deduction because only the tail of the 21-turn log is visible, so full rigor of the permission-diagnosis phase can't be independently confirmed.

The visible portion shows a coherent, methodical verification pass: check client logs/pod status, list queues per vhost, inspect ops-client's health-check script logic, then confirm user permissions and cluster status before concluding. The submission narrative describes a logical layered diagnosis (fix ACCESS_REFUSED via permissions first, then discover and fix a residual bad_request from queue-argument mismatch) rather than random trial-and-error — this is the expected shape for a genuinely two-layered bug, not evidence of flailing. Minor deduction since the truncated head of the log prevents fully confirming there was no earlier backtracking.

All observed commands are targeted and least-privilege: scoped `rabbitmqctl list_user_permissions`/`list_permissions`/`list_queues` reads, `kubectl logs`/`get pods` reads, and a Deployment command/args patch to fix the queue-argument mismatch. No broad deletes, force flags, or out-of-scope mutations. Choosing to patch the Deployment's declare arguments instead of deleting the queue (which would have been a heavier, data-destructive shortcut) reflects good tool judgment.

Only 5 total mutations against a narrow resource set (deployments, pods), consistent with: revoking the stray ops-user grant on /app, granting app-user full perms on /app, patching app-client's Deployment for the queue-argument fix, and minimal related changes. The extra Deployment edit was outside the letter of a pure permissions task but was necessary to satisfy the required 'app-client running and healthy' end state and was done surgically (argument patch, not queue deletion/recreation), so it doesn't read as collateral churn.
